summary: Miller‑remastered soundboard captures a stellar late‑90s Dead show with standout Dark Star/Other One sequences, superb Garcia on Mexicali, and solid song selection.
review: The recording is a Miller‑remastered soundboard that sounds crisp and dynamic, letting the band’s tight playing shine. The second set delivers classic highlights – Dark Star > Saint of Circumstance > Eyes of the World, followed by an inspired Other One > Dark Star > Attic run – while Garcia even slips in a memorable midi‑horn solo on Mexicali. Minor criticisms focus on aggressive accordion choices and a few flat moments, but overall the show is regarded as one of the best from this era.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance and recording across five reviews; standout songs and excellent Miller remaster push the rating into the upper‑range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller‑remastered soundboard is clear, balanced and dynamic; reviewers note no significant flaws or drop‑outs.

standout_songs: Dark Star > Saint of Circumstance > Eyes of the World
The Other One > Dark Star > Attic
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid with a highlight in Cumberland, while the second set is a tour‑de‑force of tight transitions and inspired song choices; overall pacing is praised despite a few flat moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers great midi‑horn solos on Mexicali and strong lead work throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce (Brent) and Vince’s keyboards/accordion are noted; the accordion can be aggressive and occasionally overpowers the mix.
